title: Load Balancers: The Traffic Cops of the Internet

dialogue.line: Stewie, what the heck is a load balancer?
Think of it as a traffic cop for server requests, Peter.
Traffic cop? Is it writing tickets to websites?
No, you dolt! It distributes incoming network traffic evenly.
That way, no single server gets overwhelmed.
Ah, so it keeps servers from crashing like my diet plan.
Precisely! It's crucial for high-traffic sites like online stores.
So when too many people want my famous burgers, it helps?
Exactly, Peter. It prevents server meltdowns and keeps users happy.
Good, because I melt when I’m unhappy!
